steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to compare the hardness of all three minerals: fluorite, quartz, and diamond. The correct choice must rank all three against each other using their Mohs numbers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Based on their Mohs scale numbers, diamond (10) is harder than quartz (7), and quartz is harder than fluorite (4).: Directly ranks all three minerals in a single comparison using their Mohs numbers. ✓
  • Choice B — A mineral with a Mohs number of 10, like diamond, is harder than one with a Mohs number of 7, like quartz.: Compares only diamond and quartz — fluorite is left out entirely. ✗
  • Choice C — The Mohs scale of mineral hardness can be used to order quartz, fluorite, and diamond by their ability to scratch other minerals.: Mentions all three minerals but provides no actual ranking or hardness comparison. ✗
  • Choice D — Diamond can leave visible scratches on fluorite, which is why diamond has a higher number than fluorite on the Mohs scale of mineral hardness.: Compares only diamond and fluorite — quartz is left out entirely. ✗
That matches Choice A — Based on their Mohs scale numbers, diamond (10) is harder than quartz (7), and quartz is harder than fluorite (4)..
